goal: Introduce the `ucil-lsp-diagnostics::bridge` module with an `LspDiagnosticsBridge` skeleton that tracks whether Serena is managing LSP for this daemon, and proves — via module-root `bridge::` tests — that UCIL spawns no LSP subprocesses while `serena_managed = true`, satisfying P1-W5-F03's acceptance selector `-p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::`.
plan_summary: Per DEC-0008, the bridge skeleton takes a `serena_managed: bool` in its constructor — the daemon will compute this from `PluginManager::registered_runtimes()` in a later integration WO, not here. The crate currently contains only an empty `lib.rs`. This WO adds four items: (1) a `Language` enum (Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, Java, C, Cpp — trimmed to match what the fixture projects need), (2) an `LspEndpoint` struct representing a future LSP-server connection handle (transport + target language; empty/placeholder content at this stage — F04 and F07 populate it), (3) the `LspDiagnosticsBridge` struct with `serena_managed: bool`, `endpoints: HashMap<Language, LspEndpoint>`, and `diagnostics_cache: HashMap<PathBuf, Vec<Diagnostic>>` fields per master-plan §13.3 (using std `HashMap`, not `DashMap`, since F03 does not need concurrent access; F04 can promote if needed), (4) associated accessors `is_serena_managed`, `endpoint_for(Language)`, `endpoints()`, and a `Diagnostic` placeholder type covering the minimum surface the master-plan diagram shows (file, line, severity, message). The WO introduces an `Error` enum via `thiserror` (crate convention) and a crate-wide `BridgeError`/`BridgeResult` alias pattern matching the other UCIL crates. Two module-root tests in `bridge` prove the skeleton: `bridge::test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints` and `bridge::test_bridge_without_serena_has_no_endpoints_until_f07`. The test selector frozen in the feature-list is `-p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::` (module prefix, not a single function), so any test inside the `bridge` module counts — but both are at module root per the WO-0006/WO-0010 lessons-applied convention to keep future exact-match selectors unambiguous.
scope_in: `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/lib.rs` — switch from an empty placeholder to a re-export shell per .claude/rules/rust-style.md: `pub mod bridge;`, `pub mod types;`, `pub use bridge::{LspDiagnosticsBridge, BridgeError};`, `pub use types::{Language, LspEndpoint, Diagnostic, DiagnosticSeverity};`. The existing crate-level attributes (`#![deny(warnings)]`, `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]`, `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`) stay; append one crate-level `//!` rustdoc paragraph describing the bridge's role per master-plan §13.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/types.rs` — NEW module. `pub enum Language { Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, Java, C, Cpp }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]`. `pub struct LspEndpoint { pub language: Language, pub transport: LspTransport }`; `pub enum LspTransport { /* delegated to Serena — no endpoint */ DelegatedToSerena, /* placeholder for P1-W5-F07 standalone servers */ Standalone { command: String, args: Vec<String> } }` (both variants exist; Standalone is only constructed by P1-W5-F07, never by F03). `pub struct Diagnostic { pub file: std::path::PathBuf, pub line: u32, pub column: u32, pub severity: DiagnosticSeverity, pub message: String, pub source: Option<String> }`; `pub enum DiagnosticSeverity { Error, Warning, Information, Hint }`. Every pub item has a rustdoc, every `Result`-returning pub fn has `# Errors`; this module is pure data types so no `# Errors` sections needed.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` — NEW module. `pub struct LspDiagnosticsBridge` with fields: `serena_managed: bool`, `endpoints: std::collections::HashMap<Language, LspEndpoint>`, `diagnostics_cache: std::collections::HashMap<std::path::PathBuf, Vec<Diagnostic>>`. Public API: `pub fn new(serena_managed: bool) -> Self` — constructs with both maps empty; `pub const fn is_serena_managed(&self) -> bool`; `pub fn endpoint_for(&self, language: Language) -> Option<&LspEndpoint>`; `pub fn endpoints(&self) -> &HashMap<Language, LspEndpoint>`; `pub fn insert_endpoint(&mut self, endpoint: LspEndpoint) -> Option<LspEndpoint>` — reserved for P1-W5-F07 usage but added here so the field is not dead; returns the prior entry if any. Plus `pub enum BridgeError` via `thiserror::Error` (with `#[non_exhaustive]`) containing at least one variant, e.g. `DuplicateEndpoint { language: Language }` — will be populated by F07; at F03 only the enum and one placeholder variant exist. Rustdoc on every pub item; `# Errors` section on every `Result`-returning pub fn.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` — module-root test 1: `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` or plain `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` `fn test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints() { let bridge = LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(true); assert!(bridge.is_serena_managed()); assert!(bridge.endpoints().is_empty()); assert!(bridge.endpoint_for(Language::Python).is_none()); }`. This test lives at **module root** (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) so that a future planner who freezes a per-test exact-match selector on it gets the path `bridge::test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints` rather than `bridge::tests::...` — honouring the WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0010/WO-0013 lesson. A plain `#[test]` (not `#[tokio::test]`) is sufficient since the bridge has no async work at F03.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` — module-root test 2: `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_bridge_without_serena_has_no_endpoints_until_f07() { let bridge = LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(false); assert!(!bridge.is_serena_managed()); assert!(bridge.endpoints().is_empty()); assert!(bridge.endpoint_for(Language::Rust).is_none()); }`. Also module root.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` — one additional supporting test INSIDE `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` exercising `insert_endpoint` round-trip (constructs a `LspEndpoint { language: Language::Python, transport: LspTransport::Standalone { command: "pyright-langserver".into(), args: vec!["--stdio".into()] } }`, inserts it, asserts `endpoint_for(Python).is_some()`, re-inserts and asserts the prior value is returned). The selector `bridge::tests::test_insert_endpoint_round_trip` is NOT in the feature-list oracle so the `mod tests` wrapper is acceptable for it.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml` — add `[dependencies] serde = { workspace = true }` (for `Language` derive). Keep the existing `thiserror`, `tokio`, `tracing`, `ucil-core` lines byte-for-byte.

`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml` — no `dashmap` in this WO. Master-plan §13.3 mentions `DashMap<PathBuf, Vec<Diagnostic>>` for the cache; F03 uses `std::collections::HashMap` and reserves promoting to DashMap for F04 when concurrent access lands.

Rustdoc on every `pub` item in `types.rs` and `bridge.rs`. For `LspDiagnosticsBridge::new`, document the `serena_managed` parameter explicitly (cite DEC-0008 in the doc). Every `Result`-returning pub fn has a `# Errors` section per .claude/rules/rust-style.md.
Commits follow .claude/rules/commit-style.md with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W5-F03`, `Work-order: WO-0014` trailers. Suggested commit cadence (4 commits target, each ≤200 lines per DEC-0005):
1. `build(lsp-diagnostics): add serde workspace dep for Language enum derives`
2. `feat(lsp-diagnostics): add Language, LspEndpoint, LspTransport, Diagnostic types module`
3. `feat(lsp-diagnostics): add LspDiagnosticsBridge skeleton with serena_managed state`
4. `test(lsp-diagnostics): add module-root bridge:: acceptance tests for F03`
scope_out: P1-W5-F04 (LSP JSON-RPC client) — `diagnostics::` module NOT introduced in this WO. Any `textDocument/diagnostic`, `callHierarchy/*`, `typeHierarchy/*` protocol work is deferred.
P1-W5-F05 (G7 quality pipeline feed) — not in scope; no `quality_issues` writes.
P1-W5-F06 (G4 architecture feed) — not in scope.

P1-W5-F07 (degraded-mode spawning own pyright/rust-analyzer/tsserver) — `LspTransport::Standalone` variant is introduced here as an empty placeholder so F07 can fill it without re-engineering the enum, but **no spawn logic exists at F03**. Spawning subprocesses is F07's job.
P1-W5-F08 (integration test suite) — not in scope; F03 tests are pure in-crate unit tests that construct the bridge directly with a bool.
P1-W5-F02 (Serena → G1 fusion) — depends on P1-W4-F05 which is still blocked by in-flight WO-0012 (P1-W4-F02/F08). Not touched.

Wiring the bridge into the daemon's startup sequence (reading `PluginManager::registered_runtimes()` and passing `serena_managed` at bridge construction) — reserved for the progressive-startup integration WO, same as P1-W5-F07 wiring. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` is forbidden here per the precedent set by WO-0011/WO-0013.
Dependency on `ucil-daemon` from `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` — forbidden to avoid a cycle per DEC-0008. The bridge takes a plain `bool`; the integration WO translates `PluginManager` state to the bool.

`DashMap` — master-plan §13.3 lists `DashMap<PathBuf, Vec<Diagnostic>>` but F03 does not need concurrent access. Use `std::collections::HashMap`. Promoting to `DashMap` is explicitly F04's call once real concurrent access appears.

Editing `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**` — out of scope. The bridge crate is the only surface changed.

Modifying any in-flight branch's files: WO-0006 (P1-W2-F02/F03/F06 — symbols, chunker, storage), WO-0008 (P1-W3-F01/P1-W4-F07 — lifecycle, session state), WO-0012 (P1-W4-F02/F08 — knowledge_graph CRUD + hot staging).
acceptance: P1-W5-F03: `cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::` matches at least the two module-root tests and passes them (plus the one supporting `tests::` test; 3 total).
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics` exits 0 with all tests green.
`cargo clippy -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0 (pedantic + nursery clean).
`cargo doc -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-deps` exits 0 with no broken intra-doc links.

`grep -nE 'fn test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs` — found at module root (line number lies between the module attributes and the first `mod tests {` occurrence, i.e., NOT inside the `mod tests { ... }` block).
`grep -nE 'pub enum Language' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/types.rs` — exactly one match.

No mocks of Serena, LSP servers, or MCP transport anywhere in the diff. The tests construct a plain `LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(bool)` and exercise accessor methods — no subprocess spawning, no network, no MCP, no LSP protocol.
`crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml` — new `serde` dep present; no new `ucil-daemon` dep (would cycle).
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge:: --no-fail-fast — exit 0 with ≥2 module-root tests matched and passing; the frozen feature-list selector `-p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::` is satisfied.
cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics --no-fail-fast — exit 0 with all tests (module-root + supporting `mod tests`) passing.
grep -RInE 'todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/ returns no matches.

grep -nE 'fn test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints|fn test_bridge_without_serena_has_no_endpoints_until_f07' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs returns 2 matches; both lines are outside any `#\[cfg\(test\)\] mod tests \{` block in the same file.
grep -nE 'ucil-daemon\s*=' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml returns no matches (DEC-0008: bridge must not depend on ucil-daemon).
grep -nE 'serde\s*=' crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/Cargo.toml returns at least 1 match (new dep).

scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W5-F03 — stashing crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/src/bridge.rs + types.rs must make the `bridge::` tests disappear (or fail to compile); popping must restore them. Known caveat: multi-file features may false-positive per escalation 20260415-1630; manual verification by the verifier is the documented workaround.
implementation_notes: SERENA DELEGATION INTERPRETATION (DEC-0008): the feature description's phrase 'shared socket' is interpreted as a shared *delegation* channel, not a literal socket. When `serena_managed = true`, UCIL simply does not spawn LSP subprocesses; Serena continues to manage them internally and serves semantic LSP requests via its MCP tools. This WO does NOT implement that delegation (that's F04's job via a `SerenaClient` trait added later) — it only carries the boolean flag so F04 has a data path to key on.

CONSTRUCTOR SHAPE IS LOAD-BEARING: `LspDiagnosticsBridge::new(serena_managed: bool) -> Self` is frozen per DEC-0008. Future WOs may ADD constructors (e.g. `LspDiagnosticsBridge::with_serena_client(client: Arc<dyn SerenaClient>) -> Self`) but must not break `new`. The single-parameter constructor is deliberate — F04 can introduce a builder when multiple knobs are needed.

MODULE-ROOT TESTS (WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0010/WO-0011/WO-0013 lesson): the two frozen-pattern-aligned tests `test_bridge_with_serena_managed_has_no_own_endpoints` and `test_bridge_without_serena_has_no_endpoints_until_f07` MUST live at module root in `bridge.rs`. Do NOT wrap them in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }`. The feature-list selector is a module prefix (`bridge::`) rather than an exact match, so a `bridge::tests::foo` test would technically still match — but keeping module-root discipline aligns with the project convention established by prior WOs and is what future planners will freeze if they promote any of these tests to a frozen exact-match selector.

SUPPORTING TEST IN `mod tests { }` IS FINE: the third test `test_insert_endpoint_round_trip` is not selector-frozen; wrapping it in `mod tests { ... }` is acceptable and in fact cleaner if it needs shared helpers.

NO `unwrap()` / `expect()` OUTSIDE `#[cfg(test)]`: per .claude/rules/rust-style.md. At F03 the code has no `Result`-returning fallible paths in shipped code anyway, but future additions must honour this.

CLIPPY PEDANTIC + NURSERY are already enabled at the crate level (`src/lib.rs` lines 6-8). Anticipate lints: `clippy::module_name_repetitions` (`BridgeError` in module `bridge` — allow at item level with a reason string if Biome flags; or rename), `clippy::missing_errors_doc` (populate `# Errors`), `clippy::must_use_candidate` on `is_serena_managed` (add `#[must_use]`), `clippy::doc_markdown` (backtick `LspDiagnosticsBridge`, `serena_managed`, etc. in rustdoc).

NO DASHMAP: the master plan mentions `DashMap<PathBuf, Vec<Diagnostic>>` for the cache. F03 uses `std::collections::HashMap` exclusively — no new crate dep. F04 can promote to `DashMap` when concurrent cache access becomes a real concern.

CYCLE-FREE DEPENDENCY: `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` must not depend on `ucil-daemon`. Bridge takes `serena_managed: bool` from outside. The integration WO (later) reads `PluginManager::registered_runtimes()` inside `ucil-daemon::main.rs` (or a helper crate) and passes the bool.

LSPTRANSPORT PLACEHOLDER: `LspTransport::Standalone { command, args }` is declared at F03 so F07 can populate it without touching the enum. `LspTransport::DelegatedToSerena` is the companion variant documenting Serena delegation (not used to construct endpoints at F03 — the endpoint map is empty in both cases). Both variants exist so the enum is exhaustive and avoids clippy's `enum_variant_names` trap.

DIAGNOSTIC PLACEHOLDER FIELDS: `Diagnostic { file, line, column, severity, message, source }` matches the minimum LSP diagnostic surface and has no runtime behaviour at F03 — just a data container. F04 will produce `Diagnostic` values from real `textDocument/diagnostic` LSP responses.

THISERROR CONVENTION: `BridgeError` with `#[non_exhaustive]` and at least one variant (e.g. `DuplicateEndpoint { language: Language }`). At F03 no API path actually returns `Err(BridgeError)` — that's fine; the enum exists so F04/F07 can extend it without breaking consumers. `#[non_exhaustive]` is mandatory per .claude/rules/rust-style.md.

SERDE DERIVE ON LANGUAGE: `Language` gets `serde::Serialize/Deserialize` because future features (F05 quality_issues JSON, F06 architecture deltas JSON, F08 integration-test fixtures) serialise language tags. Keep it simple: `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` with the default `lowercase` rename-all is NOT applied — the enum variants serialise with their default names (`"Python"`, `"Rust"`, …). F04 can add `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` if needed.

COMMIT CADENCE (target 4 commits, each ≤200 lines per DEC-0005): (1) `build(lsp-diagnostics): add serde workspace dep`, (2) `feat(lsp-diagnostics): add Language, LspEndpoint, LspTransport, Diagnostic types module`, (3) `feat(lsp-diagnostics): add LspDiagnosticsBridge skeleton with serena_managed state`, (4) `test(lsp-diagnostics): add module-root bridge:: acceptance tests for F03`. Combine #3 and #4 into a single commit ONLY if keeping them separate would produce a non-compiling intermediate (e.g., if `pub struct LspDiagnosticsBridge` lands without any test consuming it, `cargo build` still passes — so keep the commits separated).

WO-0012 SESSION-ABANDONMENT LESSON (verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md): produce a non-empty commit sequence in the first 30-40 minutes of the executor session. The one judgment call in this WO is the LspTransport enum shape — if it feels underspecified, default to the two variants listed in scope_in and file a clarification note in the WO's ready-for-review marker rather than stalling or silently ending the session.

WO-0008 MUTATION-GATE IS NO LONGER IN FORCE (DEC-0007): scripts/verify/mutation-gate.sh no longer runs per-WO. Reality-check.sh is the oracle — stashing bridge.rs+types.rs and re-running `cargo nextest run -p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::` must fail; popping must succeed. Per DEC-0008 and escalation 20260415-1630, per-file rollback false-positives on multi-file features are a known caveat; the verifier will document the manual check if automated reality-check is inconclusive.

WO-0013 DID NOT EDIT `ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` per the WO-0011 planner directive. This WO likewise leaves `main.rs` untouched. The daemon-level integration (reading PluginManager state → passing bool → constructing bridge) is reserved for the progressive-startup WO once WO-0008 merges.

master-plan:§13.4 lines 1424-1431 — Serena-active detection and LSP server sharing semantics
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 5 line 1759 — 'Implement ucil-lsp-diagnostics crate: lightweight bridge that connects to Serena's LSP servers'

decision:DEC-0001-WO-0002-large-commits.md — single-new-module commits may exceed soft 50-line target
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits.md — extends DEC-0001 to single-file crate modules with coherent type+impl+tests
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate.md — cargo-mutants removed; reality-check.sh is the oracle
decision:DEC-0008-lsp-bridge-via-serena-mcp-channel.md — THIS WO introduces this ADR; bridge takes bool not PluginManager; Serena LSP delegation is via MCP channel not shared socket

escalation:20260415-1856-wo-WO-0006-attempts-exhausted.md — module-root test placement lesson for frozen selectors (selector here is module-prefix, not exact-match, but discipline is still applied)
work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0013-serena-plugin-install.json — just-merged WO that flipped P1-W5-F01 (the dep for this WO); Serena is ACTIVE-capable via `ucil plugin install serena`
plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml — real Serena manifest produced by WO-0013; demonstrates what the PluginManager sees when Serena is ACTIVE (name="serena", category by dir="structural")
lessons_applied: WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0010/WO-0011/WO-0013 module-root test placement: both acceptance tests sit at module root in bridge.rs, not inside `mod tests { ... }`. The feature selector `-p ucil-lsp-diagnostics bridge::` is a module prefix (more forgiving), so the module-root discipline is belt-and-suspenders rather than strictly required — but it aligns with the project convention.

WO-0011/WO-0013 avoidance of main.rs wiring: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` stays untouched; daemon integration of the bridge is reserved for the progressive-startup WO once WO-0008 merges. This WO's forbidden_paths forbids all of `crates/ucil-daemon/**` to prevent merge-conflict with in-flight WO-0008 branches.

DEC-0001/DEC-0005 large-commit precedent: three new files (`lib.rs` update + `types.rs` + `bridge.rs`) land in three separate commits, each a coherent single-file module. If any single commit exceeds ~200 lines (unlikely at F03 scope), cite DEC-0005 in the commit body. Otherwise the default ~50-line target applies.

DEC-0007: no cargo-mutants run is part of this WO's gate. Reality-check.sh is the mutation oracle; verifier must stash bridge.rs+types.rs and confirm `bridge::` tests disappear/fail, then pop and confirm they pass.

DEC-0008: the 'shared socket' in the feature description is interpreted as Serena's existing MCP channel; the bridge skeleton takes a plain `bool` to keep `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` cycle-free from `ucil-daemon`. This WO intentionally does NOT wire to a real PluginManager — that's the integration WO's job.

WO-0012 session-abandonment lesson: executor must produce non-empty commits in the first 30-40 minutes. The single ambiguity risk here is the `LspTransport` enum shape — if it feels underspecified, default to the two variants in scope_in and proceed. Do NOT stall or end the session with a dirty tree.

WO-0008 escalation/DEC-0007 lesson: no per-WO mutation gate; reality-check is the oracle. The per-file rollback false-positive caveat (escalation 20260415-1630) means the verifier may need to document a manual two-file stash check — noted in the acceptance_criteria.

WO-0011 planner-notes directive carried forward: avoid touching `crates/ucil-daemon/**` until the progressive-startup integration WO (post WO-0008 merge). This WO honours that directive via forbidden_paths.

Phase-log invariant #1 (no mocks of critical deps): Serena, SQLite, LMDB, tree-sitter are not touched in F03 at all. The bridge takes a plain bool and constructs pure data structures — no subprocess, no network, no MCP, no LSP protocol.
planner_notes: State recap at planning time: 23/234 features passing overall; Phase-1 passing set = {P1-W2-F01, P1-W2-F05, P1-W3-F04, P1-W3-F05, P1-W3-F06, P1-W3-F07, P1-W4-F01, P1-W4-F06, P1-W5-F01}. In-flight (branches exist, features still passes=false, forbidden to re-scope here): WO-0006 (P1-W2-F02/F03/F06), WO-0008 (P1-W3-F01/P1-W4-F07), WO-0012 (P1-W4-F02/F08). Of the 25 remaining Phase-1 features with passes=false, only TWO have dependencies met AND are not on an in-flight branch: (a) P1-W3-F08 progressive-startup — explicitly deferred per WO-0013 planner-notes until WO-0008 Lifecycle merges (which has not happened yet); (b) P1-W5-F03 LSP bridge skeleton — the only unambiguously actionable feature this iteration. This WO targets P1-W5-F03 alone, spans only Week 5 of the master plan (≤1 week scope per planner workflow rule), and produces a narrow, cycle-free crate-local change that does not conflict with any in-flight branch. A single ADR (DEC-0008) was written to resolve the literal-vs-semantic interpretation of master-plan §13.4 'shared socket'; without it the executor would have no path forward because upstream Serena v1.0.0 does not expose LSP server endpoints externally. ADR is committed alongside this WO.
